~/.claude/projects/-Users-me-myproject/memory/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ MEMORY.md                  вҶҗ index file, one pointer per line
в”ңв”Җв”Җ user_role.md               вҶҗ "Backend engineer, fluent in Go, new to React"
в”ңв”Җв”Җ feedback_testing.md        вҶҗ "Integration tests must use real DB, no mocking"
в”ңв”Җв”Җ project_auth_rewrite.md    вҶҗ "Auth rewrite driven by compliance, not tech debt"
в””в”Җв”Җ reference_linear.md        вҶҗ "Pipeline bugs tracked in Linear INGEST project"

MEMORY.md sample (each line вүӨ150 chars):
- [User role](user_role.md) вҖ” Backend engineer, strong Go, new to React
- [Testing rule](feedback_testing.md) вҖ” No mocking the database in integration tests
- [Auth rewrite](project_auth_rewrite.md) вҖ” Compliance-driven, not tech debt
- [Bug tracker](reference_linear.md) вҖ” Pipeline bugs вҶ’ Linear INGEST

uv tool install "memsearch[onnx]"

в”Ӯ  ## 2026-03-28 Deploy troubleshooting           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Redis port conflict resolution:                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  1. docker-compose up вҶ’ Redis container failed  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  2. Host port 6379 occupied by another instance в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  3. Changed docker-compose.yml: "6380:6379"     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  4. Updated .env: REDIS_PORT=6380               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  5. Updated config/database.py connection       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Note: Only affects local dev. Prod unaffected. в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                 в”Ӯ

# Index markdown files
memsearch index ./memory

# Search memories
memsearch search "Redis port conflict"

# Expand a specific memory's full content
memsearch expand a3f8c1

# Watch for file changes, auto-index
memsearch watch ./memory

# Compact old memories
memsearch compact
from memsearch import MemSearch

mem = MemSearch(paths=["./memory"])
await mem.index()                          # index markdown
results = await mem.search("Redis config") # hybrid search
await mem.compact()                        # compact old memories
await mem.watch()                          # auto-index on file change
